description: Journalctl allows for complex querying and reading of the detailed binary log data collected by systemd.

commands.explanation: Starts paging through all chronologically assembled systemd logs from the beginning of recorded history.
Continually follows newly generated logs in real-time. Equivalent to 'tail -f'.
Simply prints the exact specified number of the most recent log lines.
Strictly filters tracking metadata so only logs belonging to the specified systemd unit/service are shown.
Returns logs restricted by severity level. (Levels: 'emerg', 'alert', 'crit', 'err', 'warning', 'notice', 'info', 'debug').
Isolates and prints all logs exclusively tied to the current system boot sequence.
Reviews logs exclusively attached to historical boots. (-1 is the previous boot, -2 is two boots ago).
Parses logs bounded by explicit timestamps or human-readable time intervals.
Displays the precise amount of disk space occupied by the compiled binary journal storage.
Forcefully cleans out old logs, restricting the maximum journal archive size to the provided parameter.
